title: Plants Need Fresh Air Too!
story: People actually take their houseplants outside for walks on July 27! This silly holiday encourages plant parents to give their green friends some fresh air and sunshine. Some folks put their plants in wagons, strollers, or even carry them around the neighborhood. Others simply move their plants to porches, balconies, or yards for the day. Plant lovers share funny photos online of their "plant walks" and talk about how happy their leafy companions look. It's a playful way to remember that houseplants love spending time outdoors too!
activity_question: How do people typically take their plants for walks on this holiday?
activity_choices: By throwing them in the air
follow_up_question: What do you think your houseplants would enjoy most about going outside?

title: Step Up Your Fun on Stilts!
story: People around the world grab wooden poles and strap them to their legs every July 27 for Walk on Stilts Day! This wobbly holiday celebrates the ancient art of stilt walking, where you tower above everyone else while trying not to fall over. Kids and adults practice in their backyards, parks, and playgrounds, taking careful steps and giggling when they stumble. Some brave stilt walkers organize parades or contests to see who can walk the farthest without toppling. Others just enjoy the silly challenge of being temporarily tall. It's like being a friendly giant for a day!
activity_question: What do people strap to their legs on Walk on Stilts Day?
follow_up_question: What other activities make you feel like a giant?

title: Snooze and You Lose Day!
story: The last person in your family to wake up on July 27 gets a silly punishment! National Sleepy Head Day is all about catching sleepyheads and having fun with whoever stays in bed the longest. Families might dump water on the laziest sleeper, make them do extra chores, or give them a goofy nickname for the day. Some people post funny photos of their sleepy family members online or create wake-up challenges. It's the one day when being an early bird really pays off, and hitting the snooze button could get you in trouble!
activity_question: What might happen to the last person to wake up on National Sleepy Head Day?
follow_up_question: What creative wake-up challenge would you design for your family?
